description: Swiss popular initiative to ban construction of minarets. YES (ban) won 57.5% vs NO 42.5% with 53.8% turnout. Major upset as polls predicted comfortable NO. Tests hidden anti-immigration sentiment and social desirability bias.

key_events.description: SVP launches controversial campaign poster showing black minarets piercing a Swiss flag like missiles, worn by a woman in niqab.
Government, all major parties, churches, and business groups publicly oppose the initiative. Media consensus forms against ban.
Libyan diplomatic crisis (Gaddafi hostage row with Switzerland) heightens anti-Muslim sentiment in public discourse.
Final GfS Bern poll shows 37% YES, 46% NO. All pollsters and analysts predict comfortable rejection.
Intense last-week SVP ground campaign in rural and suburban areas. Voters mobilized through direct mail and local networks.

calibration_notes: Tests extreme social desirability bias and polling failure. The largest Swiss polling miss in modern history: polls showed ~37% YES but result was 57.5%, a 20+ point error. All elite institutions opposed the ban but voters broke sharply in favor. Key dynamics: shy voter effect on culturally sensitive questions, rural-urban divide, SVP's emotionally charged visual campaign bypassing rational debate, elite-public disconnect, and the limits of establishment consensus in direct democracy.
